checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=1430100000 (2017-05-28) growing to ReceivablesNetCurrent=1684200000 (2018-05-27), +17.8%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15619800000 (FY2017) grew to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15740400000 (FY2018), only +0.8%
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, period end 2018-11-25 filing containing 2018-05-27 comparative

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=1360800000 (2016-05-29) rose to ReceivablesNetCurrent=1430100000 (2017-05-28), +5.1%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell from 16563100000 (FY2016) to 15619800000 (FY2017), -5.7%

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4530400000 (2017-05-28) increased to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=7445100000 (2018-05-27), +64.4%, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only from 15619800000 (FY2017) to 15740400000 (FY2018), +0.8%

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2841000000 (FY2017-05-29 to 2018-05-27) exceeds NetIncomeLoss=2131000000 for the same period
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2542800000 (FY2014-05-26 to 2015-05-31) far exceeds NetIncomeLoss=1221300000 for the same period

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsSold=10056000000 and SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15619800000 (FY2017) imply ~35.6% gross margin; CostOfGoodsSold=10312900000 and SalesRevenueGoodsNet=15740400000 (FY2018) imply ~34.5% gross margin, a modest and explainable shift

InventoryNet=1483600000 (2017-05-28) grew to InventoryNet=1642200000 (2018-05-27), +10.7%, versus revenue growth of only 0.8%, consistent with inventory added via the Blue Buffalo acquisition rather than organic buildup

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=1824400000 (FY2014) fell to NetIncomeLoss=1221300000 (FY2015), a -33% decline, tracking a revenue decline from SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17909600000 (FY2014) to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17630300000 (FY2015)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=29600000 (2016-05-29) declined to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=24300000 (2017-05-28), a -17.9% decrease, while ReceivablesNetCurrent grew from 1360800000 to 1430100000 (+5.1%) over the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K or NT 10-Q filings appear in the chronology; regular annual 10-K and quarterly 10-Q cadence maintained through 2018-12-19 (10-Q) and 2018-06-29 (10-K)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available for Assets, Liabilities, SalesRevenueGoodsNet, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, ReceivablesNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill spanning FY2008 through FY2018 (2018-11-25 latest instant)

top_signals: Blue Buffalo acquisition drove Goodwill up 60.8% (Goodwill=8747200000 to 14065000000) and net intangibles up 64.4% (IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4530400000 to 7445100000) between FY2017 and FY2018 while revenue grew only 0.8%, warranting scrutiny of purchase-price allocation but consistent with a disclosed large M&A transaction rather than manipulation

Receivables outpaced revenue growth in both FY2017 (receivables +5.1% vs revenue -5.7%) and FY2018 (receivables +17.8% vs revenue +0.8%), partly acquisition-driven but worth monitoring for channel-stuffing risk
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined 17.9% in FY2017 even as receivables grew 5.1%, a modest reserve-adequacy flag
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across the covered decade, indicating high earnings quality and offsetting the balance-sheet-based flags
No late filings, NT forms, or recent amendments were observed in the filing chronology near the cutoff date
